LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE [CCP 425.16] IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THIS CASE IS SUBJECT TO CCP 425.16. "LEGAL MALPRACTICE IS NOT AN ACTIVITY PROTECTED UNDER THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE." KOLAR V. DONAHUE, MCINTOSH & HAMMERTON (2006) 145 CAL.APP.4TH 1532, 1535. WHETHER PLAINTIFFS HAD AN ACTUAL OR IMPLIED-IN-FACT ATTORNEY CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH DEFENDANT IS AN ISSUE TO BE DETERMINED IN THE UNDERLYING MALPRACTICE ACTION. THE TWO PRIOR COURT ORDERS RELATING TO DISCOVERY DISPUTES ARE NOT DISPOSITIVE OF THIS ISSUE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16(C )(2) IS DENIED BECAUSE THE MOTION, ALTHOUGH LACKING IN MERIT, IS NOT FRIVOLOUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. Notice Of Moiton Andmotion To Specially Strike First Amended Complaint [Ccp 425.16]
Off Calendar Jul-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM A. LAPCEVIC OF THE ELLIS LAW GROUP LLP, TEL. NO. (916) 283-8820 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES B. WRIGHT OF THE BUCHALTER NEMER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 227-0900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP's Special Motion to strike [Ccp 425.16] is denied. Defendant fails to show that this case is subject to CCP 425.16. "Legal malpractice is not an activity protected under the anti-SLAPP statute." Kolar v. Donahue, McIntosh & Hammerton (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 1532, 1535. Whether Plaintiffs had an actual or implied-in-fact attorney client relationship with defendant is an issue to be determined in the underlying malpractice action. The two prior court orders relating to discovery disputes are not dispositive of this issue. Plaintiff's request for attorneys fees and costs pursuant to CCP 425.16(c )(2) is denied because the motion, although lacking in merit, is not frivolous. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER BAKER OF BUCHALTER NEMER, TEL. NO. 227-3655 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : Defendant's motion For Order Compel Further Resp To Discovery Special Interrogatory 16. Denied
Special Interrogatory 17. Denied in part, granted in part. If Plaintiff is withholding documents on the basis of privilege, a privilege log must be provided. The responses may be subject to further proceedings if Defendant contends that no privilege attaches to any withheld documents. Special Interrogatory 20. Granted.
Special Interrogatories 42-49. Denied. These interrogatories address more than a single subject in 12 pgs of declarations. While the declarations are broadly about a single subject, each paragraph presumably deals with a discrete fact. Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1291. Plaintiff may have to respond to additional interrogatories once these declarations are broken down into single subjects. Defendant may well be within its rights to exceed 35 special interrogatories and to require a response to each numbered paragraph in each declaration-provided each of the new interrogatories is brought for a valid purpose and not for the purpose of papering the opposing party. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant propounding additional interrogatories-each confined to single subjects. The Court makes no ruling as to whether Defendant may propound such additional interrogatories.
Form Interrogatories 9.1, 9.2, 17.1, 50.1: Granted. Requests for Admissions 1-5, 7, 9, 11, 12, 15, 17, 21, 22 and 25: Granted. Document requests 15, 22-23, 25: Denied. The Court read, but was not influenced by Defendant's Supplemental Reply Brief. The Court agrees with Defendant that the definition of "Capital Contribution Dispute" is adequately defined for discovery. Plaintiff's Objections to Evidence in the Bales Declaration are overruled. Plaintiff's Objections to the Azarmi Declaration are moot; the Court did not consider the Declaration or its exhibit. No sanctions are awarded to either party. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Tom Cohen, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/MJM
